evaluation.manager_summary: Agent completed a functional intake call collecting substance use history, insurance details, and setting a follow-up expectation. However, the call lacked warmth and rapport-building, the agent made clinical determinations about level of care rather than framing recommendations appropriately, and caller objections about job concerns were dismissed rather than explored. Multiple coaching opportunities around empathy, compliance language, and objection handling.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent launched directly into rapid-fire clinical questions without any acknowledgment of the caller's difficulty or emotional state. No greeting warmth, no use of caller's name after introduction, no rapport-building in the opening 60 seconds.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Open with acknowledgment of the caller's courage in reaching out before moving to intake questions, and use their name to build connection.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured carrier, member ID, and group number correctly. However, the verification was presented transactionally without framing it as a step toward help, and the timing was abrupt after dismissing the caller's job concern.

Agent captured substance type, frequency, quantity, and withdrawal symptoms. However, no prior treatment history was collected, and no inquiry about co-occurring mental health concerns. The screening was complete enough but not thorough.

Agent identified withdrawal symptoms (shaking) which signals medical urgency in the context of daily heavy alcohol use, but did not escalate appropriately or inquire further about severity. The recommendation for residential care was directive but not framed as a safety concern.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: When withdrawal symptoms are mentioned with heavy daily alcohol use, probe for severity and frame residential care as a safety recommendation, not just a clinical fit.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Caller is the patient themselves, confirmed by first-person language throughout the call.

When caller expressed concern about her job and needing outpatient care, agent dismissed the concern quickly with a one-line FMLA reference instead of acknowledging the legitimate barrier. No exploration of alternatives or empathetic engagement with the obstacle.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Acknowledge job concerns as legitimate before offering solutions; explore whether the caller understands FMLA or needs more support navigating that conversation.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent stated someone would be in touch but timing was vague ('probably today'), no specific appointment or callback time was set, and no ownership was assigned. Caller had to prompt for timing information.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Set a specific callback time window and confirm who will call (e.g., 'Our admissions coordinator will call you between 2 and 4 PM today').

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent crossed into diagnostic territory by stating 'you need residential, not outpatient' and 'Outpatient isn't going to be enough for where you are' — making a clinical determination about level of care appropriateness rather than framing as a recommendation for clinical assessment.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Frame level of care recommendations as 'based on what you've shared, our clinical team would likely recommend residential' rather than making definitive statements about what the caller needs.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: How long?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: How much a day?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Any withdrawal when you try to stop?

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. NovaCare Health. ID BP62051326, group GRP-855615.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We'll verify. Someone will be in touch.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: FMLA can cover that. Do you have insurance?

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: About two years. It's every day.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Half a bottle of vodka, sometimes more.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Some shaking. Nothing too bad.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Some shaking. Nothing too bad.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Based on what you're describing — daily drinking, half a bottle of vodka, withdrawal symptoms — I think you need residential, not outpatient.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: It is. You should really consider the 30-day program. That's the right fit for your situation.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'd have to figure out my job. That's a big deal.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: FMLA can cover that. Do you have insurance?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Oh. I didn't think it was that bad.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Okay. We'll verify. Someone will be in touch.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Do you know when?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Probably today.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I think you need residential, not outpatient. Outpatient isn't going to be enough for where you are.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: It is. You should really consider the 30-day program. That's the right fit for your situation.
